text.title: go-http-handlers Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to scaffold server + first handler + shutdown.

step.name: server-skeleton
step.action: Copy templates/server.go: Wire(cfg, deps) returns *http.Server with all four timeouts; Run(ctx, srv) does ListenAndServe with errgroup.
step.decision-gate: If chosen framework is net/http, drop Gin imports; otherwise adjust middleware constructors.

step.name: middleware-order
step.action: Register middlewares: Recovery, RequestID, Logger, ErrorMapper (from [[go-error-handling]]), Auth, then domain.
step.decision-gate: If Recovery isn't first, panics produce blank 500s; CI grep rejects misorder.

step.name: request-dto
step.action: Create `type XxxRequest struct { ... \`json:".." binding:"required,email"\` }`. Add a ToDomain() method that maps to model.
step.decision-gate: If a field must never come from the client (ID, timestamps), do not include it in *Request.

step.name: thin-handler
step.action: Handler does: ShouldBindJSON -> validate -> svc.X(c.Request.Context(), req.ToDomain()) -> c.JSON(status, result).
step.decision-gate: If handler exceeds 40 lines, move logic into service.

step.name: graceful-shutdown
step.action: Listen for SIGINT/SIGTERM with signal.NotifyContext; on signal, call srv.Shutdown(ctx) with 10-15s timeout; cancel root ctx after.
step.output: main.go wires Run(ctx, srv) and exits cleanly.
step.decision-gate: If srv.Shutdown returns context.DeadlineExceeded, increase drain or investigate hanging handlers.
